text:
ATLANTA, Ga. (Atlanta News First) - A Georgia State University professor just struck gold twice actually, by giving her students a massive surprise.

It was a bit of a surprise Wednesday at Georgia State for a classroom full of unsuspecting law students.

“I think the students are going to be so excited,” said Mo Ivory, Creator, Founding Director and Professor of Practice of GSU’s “Legal Life of the Olympic Games” course.

Ivory is at the head of the “Legal Life of the Olympic Games” class, teaching the ins and outs of international world-class events.

You might remember Ivory from a few months back. She taught the legal life of Atlanta native Rick Ross, and then, by surprise, the boss himself showed up.

“Rick never had a moment of pause to come. I asked… he said yes immediately,” Ivory said.

The upcoming summer games are the focus now and as far as these students know, only one envelope has boarding passes to Paris for the Summer Olympics. But thanks to some help from Delta Air Lines, everybody can draw an envelope.

“It’s an amazing experience to be able to provide this opportunity to my students,” Ivory said.

That’s right, every single student will be crossing the pond to witness what for now is just lessons but will soon be life.

“With them, the sky’s the limit. But I was not expecting to go to Paris,” one student said.

“I was stunned. That was crazy. Professor Mo is always pulling out all the stops in our classes, so I just love that they gave us tickets and a cute little beret to go with them.” said another student.

So, this next generation of attorneys will be getting some pretty cool hands-on experience before they get their hands on a law degree.

“The idea that we can put the work that we’re learning in the classroom into actual practice on the ground in Paris is just like a dream come true because, for students, that’s how they’re able to tie the concepts together,” said Ivory.

If you’re interested in sponsoring students for the Olympic Games, please contact Professor Mo Ivory atmivory@gsu.edu.

More about the course

The “Legal Life of the Olympic Games” course was launched at Georgia State Law in 2019 by Professor of Practice Mo Ivory. Since then, student participants have studied the legal life of Ludacris, Kandi Burruss, Steve Harvey and Rick Ross. The course is a part of the curriculum in the Center for Entertainment, Sports & Intellectual Property Law.

title: Georgia State class surprised with trip to Paris Olympics
description: A Georgia State University professor just struck gold twice actually, by giving her students a massive surprise.
keywords: georgia state,2024 olympics,2024 paris,2024 summer olympics,mo ivory,georgia state mo ivory,georgia state law,This Legal Life of the Olympic Games,delta
